title: Id and UTCDate: add Borrow<str> for HashMap lookup by &str
description:
## Why

Id and UTCDate derive Hash+Eq and are used as HashMap keys throughout the codebase. The impl_string_newtype! macro generates five PartialEq impls but not Borrow<str>. Without it, looking up a HashMap<Id, Chat> by a &str slice requires constructing a full Id (map.get(&Id::from_raw("x"))) instead of the natural map.get("x"). Borrow<str> is precisely the trait Rust provides for this case.

## What needs to be done

Add impl Borrow<str> for Id and impl Borrow<str> for UTCDate inside impl_string_newtype!, returning &self.0. The Borrow contract (Hash+Eq consistent with &str) is already satisfied. Add a test: construct a HashMap<Id,_>, insert with an Id, look up by &str.
